quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Area between curves (vertical)"?

options.text: All variables are functions of time, so d/dt[x²] = 2x·dx/dt, d/dt[xy] = x·dy/dt + y·dx/dt
A constant solution y = c where f(x,c) = 0 for all x; appears as horizontal segments in the slope field
Area = ∫ₐᵇ [top(x) - bottom(x)]dx; always subtract lower from upper to get positive area
d/dx[cf(x)] = c·f'(x); constants factor out of the derivative

quiz_questions.explanation: "Area between curves (vertical)" is defined as: Area = ∫ₐᵇ [top(x) - bottom(x)]dx; always subtract lower from upper to get positive area. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Area between curves (horizontal)"?

options.text: f'''(x), f⁴(x), etc.; in physics: position → velocity → acceleration → jerk → snap → crackle → pop
From a graph, check if the function approaches the same value from the left and right; if not, the two-sided limit does not exist
Use similar triangles to relate the heights and distances of an object and its shadow; differentiate to find shadow length rate
Area = ∫_c^d [right(y) - left(y)]dy; integrate with respect to y when functions are expressed as x = f(y)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Area between curves (horizontal)" is defined as: Area = ∫_c^d [right(y) - left(y)]dy; integrate with respect to y when functions are expressed as x = f(y). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Finding intersection points"?

options.text: Set f(x) = g(x) and solve; these x-values define the limits of integration
Finding a specific antiderivative by using a condition like F(0) = 3 to determine the value of C
d/dx[xⁿ] = nxⁿ⁻¹ for any real n; includes x⁻¹ = 1/x, x^(1/2) = √x, x^(2/3), etc.
Uses the midpoint of each subinterval: M_n = Σf((xᵢ + xᵢ₊₁)/2)Δx; generally more accurate than left/right

quiz_questions.explanation: "Finding intersection points" is defined as: Set f(x) = g(x) and solve; these x-values define the limits of integration. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Multiple intersection points"?

options.text: The tolerance for how close f(x) must be to L; represents the desired precision of the output
f⁻¹ reverses f: if f(a) = b, then f⁻¹(b) = a; domain of f⁻¹ = range of f, and vice versa
∫u dv = uv - ∫v du; derived from the product rule: d(uv) = u dv + v du
Split into separate integrals between consecutive intersections, taking absolute value of each

quiz_questions.explanation: "Multiple intersection points" is defined as: Split into separate integrals between consecutive intersections, taking absolute value of each. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Choosing vertical vs. horizontal"?

options.text: Use whichever method avoids splitting into multiple integrals; if curves are easier to express as x = f(y), slice horizontally
For definite integrals: convert limits from x to u (u_lower = g(a), u_upper = g(b)); no need to substitute back
Divide the plate into thin horizontal strips of width dh; each strip has approximately constant pressure ρgh and area w(h)·dh
A point where the concavity of f changes; requires f''(x) = 0 or undefined AND a sign change in f''

quiz_questions.explanation: "Choosing vertical vs. horizontal" is defined as: Use whichever method avoids splitting into multiple integrals; if curves are easier to express as x = f(y), slice horizontally. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Area Between Curves is accurate?

options.text: Mean Value Theorem: Problem Solving applies MVT to solve specific AP-style problems.
The key insight is that pressure increases with depth, so different parts of the plate experience different pressures.
Basic Derivative Rules provide shortcuts for finding derivatives without using the limit definition every time.
Area Between Curves computes the area enclosed between two functions.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Area Between Curves as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Area Between Curves?

options.text: If f(x) ≥ g(x) on [a,b], then Area = ∫ₐᵇ [f(x) - g(x)]dx.
Average Value of a Function extends the concept of arithmetic mean to continuous functions.
Success requires clear justification of setup, correct antiderivatives, appropriate use of technology, and precise interpretation of results in the given context.

Key properties: ∫ₐᵇ [f ± g]dx = ∫ₐᵇ f dx ± ∫ₐᵇ g dx (linearity); ∫ₐᵇ cf dx = c∫ₐᵇ f dx (constant multiple); ∫ₐᵇ f dx + ∫ᵇᶜ f dx = ∫ₐᶜ f dx (additivity over intervals); ∫ₐᵃ f dx = 0; ∫ₐᵇ f dx = -∫ᵇₐ f dx (reversing limits).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Area Between Curves as covered in this lesson.

When the curves cross, split the integral at intersection points and use absolute values.
Antiderivatives and Indefinite Integrals introduce integration as the reverse of differentiation.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Area Between Curves as covered in this lesson.

Drug dosing models use DEs to track drug concentration in the bloodstream.
Combined with the chain rule: d/dx[e^(g(x))] = e^(g(x)) · g'(x) and d/dx[ln(g(x))] = g'(x)/g(x).
When k > 0: exponential growth (population, investment); when k < 0: exponential decay (radioactive decay, drug clearance).

Mean Value Theorem: Applications and Consequences explores the theoretical power of MVT.
The choice of vertical vs. horizontal slicing depends on which gives simpler integrands.
The power rule: d/dx[xⁿ] = nxⁿ⁻¹ for any real n.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Area Between Curves?

options.text: Land surveying: computing the area of irregularly shaped plots bounded by curves (roads, rivers) using integration
Velocity as the derivative of position: v(t) = s'(t) tells you the instantaneous speed and direction of a moving object at any moment
Computing moments of inertia in physics: ∫x²·f(x)dx often requires integration by parts, essential for structural and mechanical engineering
Average voltage of AC power: V_avg = (1/T)∫₀ᵀ V(t)dt; for sinusoidal AC, the average over a full cycle is zero, motivating the use of RMS voltage

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Area Between Curves.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Area Between Curves?

options.text: Quality control: using normal distribution integrals to determine the probability that a manufactured part falls within specifications (Six Sigma methodology)
Algorithm analysis in computer science: O(n log n) sorting algorithms (merge sort) outperform O(n²) algorithms (bubble sort) for large datasets
Population dynamics: given a birth rate minus death rate function, calculate total population change over a decade
Economics: consumer surplus = area between the demand curve and market price line = ∫(demand function - price)dx

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Area Between Curves.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Area Between Curves applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Scientific measurement: using increasingly precise instruments to estimate a true value—smaller measurement intervals approximate the 'limit' of the quantity
Computing moments of inertia in physics: ∫x²·f(x)dx often requires integration by parts, essential for structural and mechanical engineering
Engineering: computing cross-sectional area of unusually shaped beams or channels for structural and flow analysis
Bacterial growth: E. coli doubles every 20 minutes in ideal conditions; 1 bacterium becomes ~70 billion in 12 hours (2³⁶ ≈ 6.9 × 10¹⁰)

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Area Between Curves.

options.text: Algorithm analysis in computer science: O(n log n) sorting algorithms (merge sort) outperform O(n²) algorithms (bubble sort) for large datasets
Water tank problems: given inflow rate and outflow rate functions, compute water level at any time t and find when the tank is fullest
Land surveying: computing the area of irregularly shaped plots bounded by curves (roads, rivers) using integration
Scientific measurement: using increasingly precise instruments to estimate a true value—smaller measurement intervals approximate the 'limit' of the quantity

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Area Between Curves.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Area Between Curves in practice?

options.text: Market saturation: adoption of new technology (smartphones, streaming services) follows an S-curve as the product approaches market saturation
Understanding S-curves in product adoption: inflection point marks the transition from rapid early adoption growth to market saturation deceleration
Economics: consumer surplus = area between the demand curve and market price line = ∫(demand function - price)dx
Average temperature: f_avg = (1/24)∫₀²⁴ T(t)dt gives the average temperature over a 24-hour period, used for energy consumption estimates

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Area Between Curves.
